title: Lifetimes 3
description: Structs that hold references need lifetime annotations to ensure the references outlive the struct.
instructions: Add lifetime annotations to the struct definition.
hints: Structs with reference fields must declare lifetime parameters.
Add a lifetime parameter: struct Book<'a> { ... }
Each reference field needs the lifetime: author: &'a str
explanation: When a struct holds references, it needs a lifetime parameter to ensure the references it holds are valid for as long as the struct exists. `struct Book<'a>` declares that instances of Book cannot outlive the references they contain. Without this, the struct could hold dangling references.
